title: Galarhoeus virgatus
taxonomy: Galarhoeus virgatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Arumaceae. It belongs to the genus Galarhoeus, which is a group of plants native to Southeast Asia. The plant's classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Liliopsida. More specifically, its classification is as follows: Galarhoeus virgatus is a member of the family Arumaceae, and its botanical classification is Galarhoeus virgatus (Wall.) Schott, with the family name being Arumaceae. The exact classification of Galarhoeus virgatus can be broken down into its taxonomic hierarchy.

parts: Galarhoeus virgatus, also known as the Chinese croton, is a plant species used in traditional medicine.
